title: Senior Bookkeeper & Billing Specialist (Full-Time | Remote)
description:
About the Role



We are a growing group of U.S.-based companies seeking a highly reliable, detail-oriented Senior Bookkeeper & Billing Specialist to join our team long-term.



This is a full-time, remote position working 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM CST (7:00 PM – 3:00 AM Philippine Time), aligned with U.S. business hours.



We are looking for someone who is not just a task-doer, but a thinker, communicator, and problem-solver — someone who takes ownership of their work and helps us maintain strong financial clarity across our businesses.



Key Responsibilities

- Maintain accurate and up-to-date books daily

- Manage Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable

- Send invoices and follow up with clients via
 for collections

- Produce weekly and monthly financial KPI reports across multiple companies

- Reconcile accounts and proactively identify discrepancies

- Ensure financial records are clean, organized, and audit-ready

- Communicate consistently with the team via Slack

- Take initiative to resolve issues without waiting for direction



What We’re Looking For

- 4+ years of bookkeeping experience (required)

- Strong critical thinking and problem-solving ability

- High level of attention to detail and organization

- Fluent English communication (written and verbal)

- Proactive, self-starter mindset with strong ownership

- Comfortable communicating with clients regarding payments

- Reliable, consistent, and dependable

- Experience working remotely and within structured schedules



Proficiency in:

- QuickBooks (or similar accounting software)

- Slack

- Google Sheets / Excel



Technical Requirements

- Stable, high-speed internet connection (required)

- Quiet and professional work environment

- Backup internet is highly preferred

- Who This Role Is NOT For



This role is not a fit if you:



- Prefer to be told exactly what to do without thinking through problems

- Struggle with clear and professional English communication

- Have unreliable internet or frequent power interruptions

- Are working multiple jobs during the same hours

- Are not comfortable working consistent U.S.-aligned hours

- Do not take ownership or follow through on responsibilities

- Require constant supervision to complete tasks



We are looking for someone who is dependable, proactive, and takes pride in their work.



Performance Expectations

- Books are updated daily without exception

- Weekly and monthly reports are accurate, clean, and submitted on time

- Accounts receivable follow-ups are consistent and professional

- Communication is proactive — we should not have to follow up for updates

- Issues are identified early and handled with urgency



Our Standards



We operate with a high level of accountability and professionalism.

- We value clarity, speed, and ownership

- We expect tea
mbers to communicate early and often

- We do not micromanage, but we do expect results

- Reliability is critical — your team depends on you



If you take pride in doing things right the first time and being someone your team can trust, you will thrive here.



Trial Period



All hires will go through a 7-day paid trial period to ensure mutual fit, consistency, and alignment with our team standards.

title: Technical SEO Specialist (WordPress)
description:
We are a growing US-based SEO agency hiring a Technical SEO Specialist to handle hands-on implementation across multiple WordPress client sites. This is ongoing work with room to grow.



Core Responsibilities:



- Implement and troubleshoot structured data / schema markup (JSON-LD) across various page types

- Audit and fix heading tags, internal links, redirects, and on-page technical issues

- Work inside WordPress and page builders (Elementor, WP Bakery, Divi) to execute SEO changes

- Troubleshoot indexing issues, crawl errors, Core Web Vitals, and site speed problems

- Assist with site migrations, staging environments, DNS changes, and redirect mapping

- Configure and troubleshoot SEO plugins (RankMath PRO, Yoast)

- Make theme-level edits when needed (child themes, 
p, custom CSS/JS)



Required:



- Intermediate to advanced schema markup skills (JSON-LD). Must be able to write and debug schema without relying solely on plugins, although plugin functionality of RankMath PRO may be used.

- Strong WordPress backend knowledge, including theme structure, plugin management, and troubleshooting

- Technical SEO fundamentals: crawlability, indexation, canonicals, robots.txt, sitemaps, redirect logic

- Experience with page builders (Elementor, WP Bakery, or Divi)

- HTML, CSS, and basic PHP/JS skills

- Experience with Google Search Console and at least one crawling tool (Screaming Frog, Sitebulb, etc.)



Bonus:



- WordPress developer skills (custom post types, hooks, REST API, custom themes/plugins)

- Managed hosting experience (WPEngine, Flywheel, Cloudways)

- Local SEO / Google Business Profile technical setup experience

- Comfortable using AI tools in workflow



Details:



- Remote, flexible hours (some US Central Time overlap preferred)

- Communication is exclusively through Slack. You must be comfortable with asynchronous messaging and responsive throughout your working hours.

- All tasks and deliverables are managed in ClickUp. Experience with ClickUp is a plus, but you must be willing to learn it quickly.

- This is execution-focused. We set the strategy. You implement with precision.



To Apply:



1. Briefly describe your technical SEO + WordPress experience

2. Rate your schema markup skill level and give one example of schema you have implemented manually

3. List page builders you have used

4. Note your experience with Slack and ClickUp (or similar PM tools)

5. Share your availability and desired monthly salary (USD)

title: Email Outreach Manager
description:
We buy companies and need an operator who can build and run an outbound
 machine under our brand. This is not a vanity marketing role. 



We care about three things: getting messages into the right inboxes, getting the right companies to reply, and turning those replies into qualified conversations.



How we will judge this role: The right hire understands that open rate is a signal, not the scoreboard. Bounce rate, deliverability health, positive reply rate, qualified meetings, and pipeline contribution matter more.



Responsibilities

• Own a multi-domain, always-on
 program reaching about 2,000 target companies per month.

• Protect domain health by managing inbox setup, warming, routing, verification, and deliverability troubleshooting.

• Build thesis-fit lists of companies and decision-makers, not bloated databases full of bad records.

• Use AI and automation to improve research, segmentation, personalization, testing, and reply handling without making the copy sound robotic.

• Route qualified replies fast, book meetings, and keep CRM data and follow-up steps clean.

Growth

title: Creative Strategist — E-Commerce Ads | $800–$3’200/month | Full-Time
description:
Who We Are :



We run and scale e-commerce brands aggressively. Multiple stores. Multiple markets. Real ad spend. Real revenue. We are not a dropshipping course. We are not a "side hustle." We are operators building something serious, and we are moving fast.

We need people who can keep up.



The Role : Creative Strategist — Full Time



You are the brain behind our ad creatives. You don't film. You don't edit. You think, analyze, and build the concepts that our editors execute. Your job is simple: figure out what makes people stop scrolling and buy — then systematize it.



What You'll Actually Do:



Dig through
 Ads Library and 
 daily. Find winning ads. Identify patterns in hooks, angles, formats, and offers.

Analyze our own ad performance data. Understand what's working, what's dying, and why.

Build complete creative briefs for video editors — hook, visual identity, concept, angle, script structure. Nothing vague. Everything specific.

Generate a minimum of 20 new ad concepts per week across multiple products.

Collaborate directly with our media buyers to align creatives with what the data is telling us.

Iterate fast. A creative bombs? You learn from it and brief a better one within 48 hours.



Requirements :



- Solid understanding of
 Ads and 
 Ads ecosystems — how the algorithm works, what performs, why.

You know the difference between a hook and a headline. Between an angle and a concept. Between a format and a format that converts.

- Analytical AND creative. You can read numbers AND write a brief that gives a video editor chills.

- Fluent written English. No exceptions.

- Fast. We don't wait days for a brief. We don't wait a week for a revision. Speed is part of the job.

- Proactive. You don't wait to be told what to do. You see a problem, you flag it. You see an opportunity, you move on it.



Who This Is NOT For :



People who need to be micromanaged to produce output

People who "think they understand ads" but have never actually studied creatives seriously

People who are passive, slow, or who treat deadlines as suggestions

People applying to 50 jobs at once just to get any paycheck

People who will disappear after 3 months when something better comes along



Who This IS For :



Someone obsessed with what makes people buy — not theoretically, but practically

Someone who has spent real time inside
 Ads Library or 
 Creative Center analyzing ads

Someone who takes pride in their output and gets genuinely bothered by average work

Someone who wants to grow — not just do the same tasks forever, but actually learn, improve, and take on more

Someone who treats their work like a craft, not a transaction



Compensation :



$800 – $1,200/month based on experience and output

Full-time (40 hours/week)

Paid monthly, reliably, on time — every time



Growth Opportunity :



This is not a dead-end position.

We are scaling fast. The brands grow, the team grows, the responsibility grows. If you perform, you will be given more — more ownership, more visibility, more compensation. We have promoted people from junior roles to leading entire creative departments. We invest in people who invest in the work.

If you're looking for a long-term home where your growth has no ceiling — this is it.



How to Apply:



- Send your application to the listing with the following:

- Start your first sentence with the word "Pineapple" — if it's not there, your application will not be read. This tells us you - - actually read everything above.

- Tell us in 3–5 sentences: why you specifically are the right person for this role. Not generic. Not copy-pasted. Specific.

- Share one example of an ad you think is strong right now (any product, any platform) and explain in 2–3 sentences why it works.

- Your availability to start.



Applications without the keyword or with generic copy-paste intros will be deleted immediately.

We review applications fast. We move fast. If you're serious — apply now.

title: Expert Apparel Product Designer & Tech Pack Developer (Technical Clothing)
description:
We are looking for a highly experienced apparel product designer to work with our brand long-term to develop professional tech packs for our clothing products.



This is NOT a basic Illustrator job. We are specifically looking for someone with real product design and development experience in apparel, who can contribute ideas and improve product functionality — not just draw what we say.



Our brand focuses on technical performance clothing with sophisticated styling, so attention to detail and garment construction knowledge is critical.



You will work closely with our founder to turn product concepts into factory-ready tech packs that can go directly into production.



Key Responsibilities



• Develop detailed, factory-ready tech packs for apparel products

• Convert rough ideas, sketches, or references into professional garment designs

• Suggest construction improvements and product enhancements

• Select appropriate fabrics, trims, and components

• Create measurement specs and size charts

• Produce clear garment construction details (stitching, seams, panels, etc.)

• Prepare production-ready files for manufacturers

• Help refine designs to ensure they are practical and manufacturable



Required Experience



Applicants MUST have experience in apparel product design, not just graphic design.



Required skills:



• Experience designing technical apparel / sportswear / outerwear

• Strong understanding of garment construction and manufacturing processes

• Ability to build professional tech packs used by factories

• Knowledge of fabrics, trims, stitching, and garment engineering

• Experience working directly with clothing manufacturers

• Advanced skills in Adobe Illustrator



What We Are NOT Looking For

Please do NOT apply if you only:



• Trace garments in Illustrator

• Create basic fashion sketches

• Have no experience with real product development

• Have never worked with clothing factories



We are looking for someone who understands how garments are actually made.

title: Social Media Head
description:
Social Media Manager – Goldfish App (Remote, Part-Time)



We're quite possibly looking for a long-term creative partner, not a short-term hire. Strong performers will have opportunities to grow with us across future projects.



About Goldfish:

Goldfish is a social productivity iOS app built for college students — think Strava, but for studying. We help students build better study habits through accountability, streaks, and a community of peers. We're a small, ambitious startup team based in the United States, and we're looking for a creative social media manager to help us build our brand from the ground up.



The Role:

We're hiring a part-time Social Media Manager to own our presence on Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn. This is a ground-floor opportunity — our accounts are early-stage, so you'll be building the content engine from scratch. If you love short-form video, understand Gen Z culture, and can make studying feel exciting, this is your role.



What You'll Do:

Create and post short-form video content (Reels and TikToks) at least 3–5x per week:

Manage and grow our Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn accounts

Write captions, develop content ideas, and stay on top of trending audio and formats

Engage with our community (comments, DMs, reposts)

Track basic performance metrics and share weekly updates with the team



What We're Looking For:

Strong creative instincts — you come to us with ideas, not the other way around

Proven experience creating short-form video content (please share a portfolio or your own social accounts)

Strong understanding of TikTok and Instagram Reels trends

Excellent written English — captions matter

Self-directed and reliable; we're a small team and need someone who takes ownership



Bonus: experience in edtech, productivity, or student-facing brands



Logistics:

Hours: ~8 hours per week, flexible schedule

Compensation: Hourly to start (trial period), moving to monthly once we establish a strong working relationship

Location: Fully remote

Timezone: Overlap with US Eastern time preferred but not required



To Apply:

Send us a brief note on why you're a good fit, plus links to content you've created. Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.
salary: Ideally pay p. video, but ~$5/hour start

title: Marketing Production and Funnel Manager  — Full Time-Financial Education Coaching Brand (Finance/Mindset)
description:
Marketing Production and Funnel Manager



About us



The Profit Room is a financial education company that helps people create supplemental and replacement income through trading and investing. We build courses, mentorship, and training experiences that help people grow financially and create more freedom in their lives.



We are looking for a Marketing Production and Funnel Manager who can help bring our ideas to life through strong creative execution, polished assets, and marketing buildout.



Who this role is for



This role is for a builder with taste.



You know how to take ideas and turn them into polished marketing assets. You are strong in Canva, video editing, page layout, and funnel-related production. You understand how content should look, feel, and flow in a way that supports lead generation and sales.



You do not need to be the ad strategist, though you should understand what makes creative and funnel pages work.



What you will own

???? Funnel page and landing page production

???? Webinar decks, PDFs, workbooks, and templates

???? Marketing campaign assets across social,
, and funnels

???? Brand consistency across creative output

???? AI-assisted marketing production support

???? Organized file management and asset delivery



Main responsibilities

???? Edit short-form videos for social media and ads

???? Edit long-form content for YouTube, trainings, or promotions

???? Build or support landing pages and funnel pages in GoHighLevel or similar platforms

???? Create decks, workbooks, PDFs, and branded marketing templates in Canva

???? Help organize creative assets for campaigns and launches

???? Work with leadership to turn rough ideas into polished deliverables

???? Maintain brand consistency across all creative materials

???? Support marketing production using AI tools when helpful

???? Stay organized with timelines, revisions, and deliverables



What success looks like



???? Content looks cleaner, stronger, and more premium

???? Funnel pages and creative assets are produced faster

???? Video edits improve in quality and hook strength

???? Webinar and campaign materials are more polished

???? Marketing output becomes more consistent

???? Leadership can move faster because creative production is no longer bottlenecked



Required skills



???? Strong Canva skills

???? Strong short-form video editing skills

???? Good sense of layout, branding, and visual storytelling

???? Ability to create polished presentations and downloadable assets

???? Familiarity with GoHighLevel or similar funnel/page-building platforms

???? Strong written English and clear communication

???? Ability to manage deadlines and revisions well

???? Ability to take direction and improve rough ideas



Preferred experience



???? Experience with coaching, education, info-product, or personal brand businesses

???? Experience building or supporting landing pages and funnel pages

???? Experience creating ad creatives and marketing assets

???? Familiarity with Meta-style creative, YouTube content, and webinar promotions

???? Understanding of what makes content convert

???? Comfort using AI tools for speed, drafts, and production support



What kind of person fits this role



???? You move fast without being sloppy

???? You care about quality and details

???? You can take feedback without getting defensive

???? You understand that pretty design alone is not enough

???? You like building things that help a business grow

???? You can work in a fast-moving company with many moving parts

???? You are creative, organized, and dependable



Schedule and compensation



This role will begin as a paid part-time trial and can grow into a full-time salaried role.



Target starting compensation range

???? $800 to $1,200 per month based on skill, experience, and trial performance

title: Video Editor
description:
We’re looking for a video editor who can create clean, aesthetic content with a simple and polished style, focusing on subtle slide-ins or pop-in transitions and incorporating a slow pan or zoom toward the subject for a smooth visual flow. 



The ideal candidate should use only high-quality images and clips, ensuring that no blurry or low-resolution visuals are included, and must carefully match all visuals to the provided script for consistency and clarity. You will be expected to add on-screen text that highlights key details such as names, dates, and important information, while also double-checking all spelling for accuracy. 



Consistency is important, so you must follow the same font style and background color as the provided sample. Applicants are required to complete a 20-second unpaid demo to showcase their editing style and attention to detail before being considered for the role. 



This role requires producing 14–16 minutes of edited video per week. The pay for this position is $3 per minute of finished video.

title: Real Estate Transaction Coordinator / Listing Coordinator (3)
description:
What is it like to work with us? LISTEN TO OUR TEAM! 



We’re hiring a highly organized, systems-minded Real Estate Transaction Coordinator.

This role is for someone who lives in the details, obsesses over contract deadlines, and can manage a complex transaction pipeline without a single ball being dropped. You aren't just "checking boxes"—you proactively identifies gaps in the contract-to-close process, automates repetitive tasks, and ensures our clients and partners experience a seamless, "magic 
nt" closing every single time.



IMPORTANT: Please read the entire job listing carefully. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



ABOUT THE TEAM

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and are dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



Our Core Values:



DELIGHT THE CLIENT: We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.



MASTER YOUR CRAFT: We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.



FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS: When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.

We are building an exceptional place to work with strong systems and high standards, not just managing tasks for a real estate business.



ABOUT YOU

Do you think in priorities, timelines, and dependencies? Do you enjoy turning a ratified purchase contract or listing agreements into a structured project plan with clear deadlines? Are you the type of person who catches a missing signature or a math error on a Closing Disclosure (CD) before anyone else sees it? Do you take pride in building clean systems, documenting SOPs, and keeping a team aligned without needing constant supervision? Do you love to communicate quickly and help keep the project on track?

If you are a high-level operator who wants to own the "engine room" of a high-volume real estate team, keep reading.



RESPONSIBILITIES

Transaction & Listing Management

Contract-to-Close Ownership: Manage every aspect of buyer and seller transactions from signed purchase agreement to closing using Skyslope and GoHighLevel.

Offer Packet Coordination: Prepare an offer packet draft with precise signature blocks and routing, and submit to the broker for review. 



Compliance & Auditing: Submit all required documentation to the office broker; ensure 100% file compliance at direction of the responsible broker by monitoring for no missing disclosures.

Closing Logistics: Coordinate with Attorneys, Lenders, Title officers and brokers; collect CD/ALTA statements for broker accuracy review against the commission agreement.



Project & Operations Management

System Building: Spot inefficiencies in the workflow and design/implement improvements or automations to reduce manual effort.



SOP Documentation: Document workflows and create step-by-step guides so the process is repeatable and scalable.

KPI Reporting: Maintain and update the "Deal Tracker" G-Sheet and GHL Opportunity values to ensure leadership has accurate data at all times.



The "Connective Tissue": Act as the bridge between leadership, agents, and clients to ensure no ball is ever dropped.



REQUIREMENTS



Experience & Capability



1. Must have extensive experience in real estate transaction coordination, transaction management, or as a high-level operations associate.

2. Preferred BPO Experience: Previous background in BPO Customer Service is highly preferred.

3. Project Management Experience: Comfortable managing 20+ active files simultaneously with varying deadlines.

4. Automation Mindset: Experience automating workflows (or a deep understanding of how to design them) is highly preferred.

5. Technical Proficiency: Working knowledge of Skyslope and GoHighLevel (GHL) is strongly preferred but not required. Fluent in AI tools (Gemini/Claude/OpenClaw) to improve output speed.



Work Style & Standards

Extremely Detail-Oriented: You find the needle in the haystack every time.

Near-Native English: Exceptional written and spoken communication skills.

Proactive Ownership: You don’t wait to be told something is broken; you anticipate it and fix it before it breaks.

Speed & Urgency: You move with a sense of purpose and meet every deadline, no excuses.



Technical Requirements

A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION is required (at least 150mbps).

Must be available to respond to messages within a 12-hour daily window (standard core hours 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM EST).

Must be flexible to work 2–4 hours on weekends as needed for urgent business requirements



APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS



STEP 1: Application

Please submit your application HERE 

To ensure you have read through the requirements, please write the phrase "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field of the application.



STEP 2: Initial Interview

If your application indicates you might be a great fit, you will be invited to participate in a quick, 10- to 15-minute initial screening interview.



STEP 3: Case Interview

Candidates who pass the initial screening will be invited to a 1-hour case interview. A case interview is an interactive session where we present you with a real-world scenario or business challenge relevant to the role. Rather than just talking about your past experience, this allows us to see your problem-solving process, technical logic, and how you tackle practical situations in real-time.



STEP 4: Reference Check

This is the final step before an official offer is extended. The final candidate(s) must provide one former employer/lead reference and one character reference.



STEP 5: The 90-Day Trial Period

If an offer is made and accepted, your employment will begin with a 90-day PAID trial period. This period is dedicated to ensuring a strong mutual fit—both for the responsibilities of the role and alignment with our core values. 

During these 90 days, we will provide you with all the necessary training, onboarding, and support you need to succeed and thrive on our team. You will be expected to master the Skyslope/GHL workflow, meet all transaction deadlines, and begin documenting/improving our internal systems. A successful trial results in a permanent invitation to the team.



Please submit your application HERE >>

description:

You will be supporting solopreneurs with their businesses' social media, content creation, video/audio editing, and possibly some VA roles like administrative tasks, and creating systems for their businesses. You’ll work with 2-4 clients at a time, and I provide guidance to ensure smooth collaboration between you and your clients.

nts

I find you your clients

 me

 coming from:

title: Video Editor + Content Research
description:
VIDEO EDITOR + CONTENT RESEARCH (FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM)



I run an e-commerce business and manage multiple social media pages across different niches. I’m looking for a full-time Video Editor + Content Researcher to join my team long-term.



This role is for someone who can edit short-form videos in CapCut, follow references closely, work fast, and understand why content goes viral.



MAIN TASKS



1) Video Editing (core task)

 • Edit short-form videos in CapCut

 • Work from my footage, my references, and my system

 • Recreate videos with the same pacing, cuts, hooks, text style, sounds, and structure

 • Replace hooks, text, sounds, and small details where needed

 • Prepare final versions ready for posting



2) Content Research

 • Find fresh viral videos, hooks, and trends in different niches

 • Collect strong reference videos

 • Understand why a video performs well

 • Send organized research into the correct workspace



TRAINING



Full training will be provided for:

 • my editing system

 • my content research system

 • my workflow and process



But you must already:

 • know CapCut

 • have at least medium-level editing skills

 • have real editing experience

 • understand short-form content structure

 • understand what makes a video perform well



Beginners will not be considered.



IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS



Because all content is created and checked on Apple devices, you must have at least:

 • a MacBook, or

 • an iPhone



This is mandatory because colors, fonts, and final look can differ across devices.



If you do not have a MacBook or iPhone, do not apply.



You must also:

 • send your previous work examples

 • state which MacBook model or iPhone model you use



WHAT I’M LOOKING FOR

 • Good CapCut skills

 • Experience with short-form content

 • Comfortable working with voiceovers, text overlays, pacing, cuts, and medium-level editing tools in CapCut

 • Able to follow references closely, not freestyle

 • Good research ability

 • Fast worker with a fast device

 • Comfortable with routine and repetitive work

 • English level good enough to fully understand instructions and video content



WORKLOAD



This is not random editing from scratch.



You will work mainly from:

 • my footage

 • my references

 • my system



The workflow is built for speed and can reach 120+ videos per week.

Not all videos are built from zero, but this is still a lot of routine, repetitive work, so you must be comfortable working fast and consistently.



PAY

 • Base rate at the start: $1,000/month for 172 hours

 • Equivalent hourly rate: $5.81/hour

 • At the beginning, compensation will be fixed salary only

 • Later, there will be an opportunity to move to salary + percentage, depending on the value, creative ideas, and results you bring

 • In the future, there may also be an opportunity to become a partner on some projects and share profit directly



SCHEDULE

 • Tuesday to Saturday

 • 06:00 AM to 02:00 PM Philippines time

 • 8 hours/day

 • Fixed schedule



Other days and other working hours are not being considered.



TO APPLY



Start your application with the word: PINEAPPLE



Also include:

 1. Your MacBook / iPhone model

 2. Your work samples



A short paid test task will be required before final selection.

infographics, breakdown guides, and side-by-side frameworks aimed at B2B marketers and revenue leaders.

This is a thinking and writing role. Design is handled separately. Your job is the angle, the structure, and the copy — d
 how to break an idea down so it lands immediately with a senior B2B audience.



????What we're looking for:



You have a solid understanding of B2B marketing and AI tools — enough to write confidently without being briefed from scratch every time



You write for decision-makers — B2B marketers, demand gen leads, and revenue leaders — and you know what actually resonates with that audience

title: Like to have fun and Travel? Come be a Travel Assistant for a company executive –1 Week in Dumaguete (All Expenses Paid)
description:
Like to have fun and Travel? Come be a Travel Assistant for a company executive – 1 Week in Dumaguete (All Expenses Covered including flights)



We’re an American company specializing in E-commerce and our VP Of Marketing is traveling to Dumaguete in May to open a company and start a local team. He’s a busy guy so you’ll be there to help planning with seeing apartments/offices/houses but also planning where to eat, hotels, and even nightlife if there’s any. 



The job lasts 1 week and you’ll be paid USD$100/day [We pay you everyday so you know you are getting paid] - You will work the entire day and then can go sleep at home or your hotel (we will cover) + Potential for a $500 bonus at the end.



Benefits:

1- $100/day paid on a daily basis so you know you’re 100% getting paid for all your work. 

2- All flights/meals/hotels covered by our corporate account.

3- This could in the future lead to a full time job in Dumaguete since we are opening a branch of our company there if our VP likes the town, and sees potential, etc.



Requirements:



1- You are responsible to plan everything and basically be his personal assistant for the entire week that he’s there - it’ll be a pretty demanding week since it includes seeing houses/offices but also getting to know the city.

2- Just for this week of work - the job is more like a paid friend + Assistant which is why we’re offering USD$100/day because it’s demanding. 

3- On part, you need to help us convince him to move to Dumaguete since he’ll be the one running the branch of our company in Philippines and he’s not sure if it’s a good idea to move there. Our plan is to open an office in 2027, we will personally offer a $500 BONUS at the end of the week if he chooses to stay in Dumaguete to open our office there and manage our local employees.



Tentative Dates: Friday, May 29 to Friday, June 5

title: Revenue Manager
description:
About Us



We are a fast-growing Airbnb management company based in Canada managing 30+ properties, with plans to scale aggressively.



We are looking for a Senior Revenue & Pricing Manager to take full ownership of pricing, occupancy, and revenue performance across our portfolio.



This is a high-impact role directly tied to company growth.



Compensation



$500 CAD/month base salary

Additional performance bonuses based on occupancy and revenue growth

Top performers can earn up to $800–$1000/month

Long-term role with growth opportunities



Schedule



4 PM – 12 AM Mountain Time (Canada)

Monday to Sunday (flexible day off)



Role Objective



Maximize bookings and revenue across all properties.



You are not here to manage PriceLabs. You are here to own performance.



Responsibilities



Own pricing strategy across 30+ listings

Manage and optimize PriceLabs (advanced usage)

Monitor booking pace and adjust pricing daily

Identify underperforming listings and take action

Analyze competitors and market demand

Optimize occupancy, ADR, and RevPAR

Adjust minimum stays, discounts, and pricing rules

Work with the team to improve listing performance

Provide weekly performance insights and action plans



KPIs



Occupancy rate (target: 70%+)

Revenue growth month-over-month

Reduction in vacant nights

Response time to underperformance (24–48 hrs)



Requirements



1–3 years experience in Airbnb or STR pricing

Experience managing 10+ listings

Strong experience with PriceLabs

Strong understanding of occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, and booking lead time

Strong analytical thinking

Fluent English

Reliable internet and laptop



What We’re Looking For



Thinks like a revenue manager, not a VA

Takes ownership of results

Makes decisions independently

Understands supply, demand, and seasonality

Can explain why pricing decisions are made



Not a Fit If You



Only use PriceLabs automation

Need constant direction

Have no STR experience

Cannot analyze data or competitors



Bonus



Experience with VRBO or ----------

Experience managing 20+ listings

Experience with PMS tools (Hospitable, Guesty, etc.)



How to Apply



Send:



Your resume and OnlineJobs profile

A short paragraph explaining your STR pricing experience

Answer this question:



One of our listings has 40% occupancy this month. What steps would you take to improve it?



IMPORTANT



Start your application with the word “Revenue”.

title: IG Outreach Specialist
description:
We're looking for a disciplined outreach specialist to manage our DM pipeline. Your job is simple but critical: send high-volume manual DMs to leads without getting accounts flagged. You focus entirely on getting the message delivered safely and accurately.



**Key Responsibilities**



- Send DMs, custom-typing/variate each message to keep the tone natural, no copy-pasting.

- Verify each prospect before sending, confirm they're a valid lead and that we haven't already messaged them.

- Follow batching protocols, send in controlled batches with breaks in between to protect account health.

- Make small, natural variations to the script every few messages to avoid automation detection.

- Mix in human activity between DMs.

- If a prospect replies, escalate it immediately



**Requirements**



- Disciplined, you can follow a workflow all day without cutting corners.

- Safety-first mindset, you understand that speed means nothing if the account gets banned.

- Detail-oriented, you never skip a check.

- Good English, enough to make natural variations to scripts so messages don't look robotic.



**KPIs**



- Daily DM Volume

- Account Health (zero blocks or flags from your activity)

- Escalation Speed (time taken to flag a reply)

title: Instagram Virtual Assistant | Multiple Accounts | Phone-Only | Full-Time Remote
description:
We are an established, profitable business hiring a full-time Instagram Virtual Assistant to manage 4 to 8+ accounts daily,  entirely from your phone.



No computer needed. No laptop needed. Just your iPhone, a stable connection, and the ability to show up and execute at a high level every single day.



We have real revenue, real structure, and real systems. We pay in USDT. We're building a team around people who are reliable, sharp, and serious about their work. If that sounds like you, keep reading.



This Is Not a Generic VA Role



We're not hiring someone to do random tasks or reply to a few DMs. We need someone who can own multiple Instagram accounts, run them cleanly every day, and hold themselves to a high standard without being micromanaged.



We have SOPs. We have clear guidelines for every account. You won't be left guessing what to do. But we expect you to show up, execute, and care about the quality of your work the same way we care about ours.



What Your Day Actually Looks Like



You open your phone and check your accounts. You review what needs to be posted, confirm the content matches the right account, format, and caption style. You post on schedule. You run through each account's engagement playbook: commenting, liking, story viewing, DM conversations. You check metrics and note anything that looks off. You log everything in your tracking sheet. At the end of your shift, you send a clean report of what was done and what needs attention.



Then you do it again tomorrow. And the day after that.



The people who thrive here are the ones who find satisfaction in doing consistent, high-quality work, not people who get bored after a week.



Your Responsibilities



- Manage 4 to 8+ Instagram accounts daily, each with its own branding, voice, and schedule

- Post the right content at the right time in the right format (Reels, stories, carousels, feed)

- Handle daily engagement: commenting, liking, story viewing, follow/unfollow based on each account's instructions

- Manage DMs across accounts, including scripted conversations that drive engagement

- Research competitors and trends to support content strategy

- Track all daily actions in Google Sheets (mobile app works fine)

- Monitor performance metrics, flag issues, and suggest improvements

- Understand Instagram's content policies and know how to maximize reach without getting flagged

- Keep all account information, content, and business details strictly confidential



What You Need



- iPhone 11 or newer. Everything runs from your phone. Multiple iPhones are a plus but not mandatory.

- Reliable internet connection. If your connection drops regularly, this won't work.

- 4 to 6 hours per day, 5 to 6 days per week. We're flexible on timezone as long as your schedule is consistent.

- Strong written English. You'll write captions, handle DMs, and send daily reports. Quality matters.

- Ability to follow detailed instructions the same way, every time, across multiple accounts.

- Comfort working with content that may not be suitable for all audiences. We'll discuss this further in the application.

- Real organizational skills. Managing 4 to 8+ accounts means 4 to 8+ sets of rules, posting schedules, and brand voices. If that sounds overwhelming, this isn't the role. If it sounds like a challenge you'd enjoy, keep going.



What We'd Love to See (Not Required, But It Helps)



- Experience managing 3+ Instagram accounts at the same time

- Caption writing and content posting experience

- DM management experience

- Competitor research and trend analysis

- Google Sheets reporting (tracking, formulas, daily logs)

- Understanding of how the Instagram algorithm works

- Experience working with accounts in niche or sensitive categories



What We Pay



$300 to $1500+ USD per month, depending on your experience, how many accounts you can handle well, and the results you deliver. Raises and bonuses are performance-based and built into the role. We don't cap what you can earn. If you produce results, your compensation grows.



We are not looking for the cheapest hire. We are looking for the best one.



Where This Can Go



This isn't a dead-end position. The right person will take on more accounts, earn more, and eventually lead a team of account managers. That growth path is real, it's earned through performance, consistency, and trust, but it's there for anyone who wants it.



Several people on our current team started where you'd be starting. They stayed because the work is stable, the pay is fair, and the growth is real.



Who Should NOT Apply



- You're not available 4 to 6 hours a day, 5 to 6 days a week

- You don't have an iPhone 11 or newer

- Your internet drops regularly

- You're careless with details or forget things easily

title: Video Editor - Short-Form, Long-Form & Ad Creatives (Part Time, Ongoing, Full Time Potential)
description:
Video Editor - Short-Form, Long-Form & Ad Creatives (Part-Time, Ongoing)



About Us

We are Ambition Avenue, a financial consulting brand for entrepreneurs based in Belgium. We create educational content about business finance, tax optimization, and investing, published on Instagram,
, and YouTube.

We are growing fast and need a dedicated video editor who can handle consistent, high-volume output while maintaining quality. This is an ongoing, long-term position, not a one-off project.





The Role

You will be our primary video editor, responsible for three types of content:

1. Short-form content (Reels / Shorts): weekly batches of vertical videos for Instagram and
. Fast-paced, engaging edits with text overlays, zoom-ins, and burned-in Dutch subtitles.



2. Long-form content (YouTube): weekly videos. Horizontal format, clean editing with chapter breaks, overlays, and polished color grading.



3. Ad creatives: one large batch every 3 to 4 months. Each batch involves stitching multiple hook clips onto ad body videos and end cards. This is mostly repetitive assembly work.



About 70% of our content is talking head. But we also create goofy, funny, and creative format videos. The style can vary.

From each long-form video, we also want you to create 2 to 3 short-form clips by picking the best moments yourself.



How We Solve the Language Barrier

- Our content is recorded in Dutch (Flemish). You do NOT need to speak Dutch. Here is how it works:



1. Pre-cut rough edit: You receive footage already cut in Descript. All content decisions are made. Mistakes and dead air are removed. Your job is to make it look and feel great.



2. English transcript: You receive an English transcript so you understand the content, flow, and key moments.



3. Dutch transcript: You receive a Dutch transcript for adding burned-in subtitles.



4. Edit notes (sometimes): For some videos, Robin will include 2 to 4 lines of specific instructions. For others, you use your own judgment.



This system is designed so you can edit effectively without understanding Dutch.



What We Expect From You

- Consistent quality across all deliverables, every video should feel on-brand

- Clean, professional editing with your own creative input

- Ability to follow a brand guide (colors, fonts, subtitle style) while bringing your own ideas

- Proactive communication: if something is unclear, ask before you start editing

- Reliable availability: we need someone who can handle consistent weekly volume

- Willingness to learn our style and improve based on feedback

- From long-form content, the ability to independently identify the best moments for short-form clips



Requirements

- Proficient in video editing software (CapCut, Premiere Pro, or equivalent)

- Experience editing short-form vertical content (Reels, Shorts)

- Experience editing long-form YouTube content

- Strong sense of pacing and retention: you understand how to keep viewers watching

- Comfortable with text overlays, zoom effects, lower thirds, and subtitle styling

- Reliable internet connection and ability to work with large files via Google Drive

- Good written English for communication



Nice to have (not required):

- Experience with ad creative editing

- Basic motion graphics or After Effects skills

- Experience with color grading



What You Get From Us

- A clear, organized workflow: you always know exactly what to do and when

- A complete brand package with all colors, fonts, and style references

- Pre-cut footage so you can focus on creative editing, not content decisions

- English transcripts for every video so the language barrier is eliminated

- Event pictures, b-roll footage, logos, and visual assets provided for you to use

- Consistent, ongoing work: this is a long-term partnership, not project-based

- Fair, on-time monthly payment

- Open and flexible communication via
, WhatsApp, or a quick call whenever needed

- If you need tools like stock music or editing platforms, we can look into providing access

- Room to grow: as we scale, there are opportunities for more editing work, marketing insights, graphic design tasks, or even physical collaboration if everything goes well



Timeline & Contract

- We start with a structured testing phase to make sure we are a good fit:



Test 1: One short-form video. Paid test task.

Test 2: 5 short-form videos, 1 long-form video, and 1 ad creative.

Full contract: If both tests go well, a guaranteed 3-month contract. Content delivered to you on Friday, finished edits due by Wednesday of the following week.



Weekly volume after onboarding: at least 4 short-form videos + 1 long-form video. Ad batches every 3 to 4 months.



Compensation

Compensation is negotiable based on experience, speed, and quality. We are looking for a fair arrangement that works for both sides. Payment is monthly.



How to Apply

To apply, please send the information below to 
 :



1. Your portfolio or links to 3 to 5 edited videos (must include at least one short-form vertical video and one long-form horizontal video)

2. A short message about your experience and which editing software you use

3. Your availability (how many hours per week you can commit)



Serious applicants only. We will send a paid test task to shortlisted candidates before making a final decision.

title: General Office Admin
description:
About FenceUSA:

-We are a fast-growing fence, deck, and railing company built on family legacy and high-stakes execution. We combine the personal trust of a local family business with the professional systems of a modern, scaling company. Our core values are Caritas, Optimism, Growth, Teamwork, and Win. We are looking for a dedicated Admin to act as a "second brain" for our Office Manager, Cynthia, and help coordinate our installation pipeline.



The Role:

-This is not just data entry. You will be a critical part of our operations team, ensuring our fence, deck, and railing projects move from contract to completion without friction.



Hours:

-9am-5pm eastern standard time.



Key Responsibilities:

-Customer Communication: Calling customers to provide updates, confirm schedules, and ensure a world-class experience.

-Operations Support: Coordinating with Cynthia to execute installation logistics.

-Tech Stack Management: Heavy use of GoHighLevel (GHL) for CRM/automation, and Google Sheets for tracking.

-Material Coordination: Using our internal software to generate and verify material lists for crews.



Mandatory Requirements:

-Exceptional English: You must have near-native fluency with a very minimal accent. You will be speaking with American customers daily.

-Tech Literacy: You must be a power user of GHL, and Google Sheets.

-Core Value Fit: You must be optimistic, growth-oriented, and a team player.

-Direct Communication: We value brevity and action.



How to Apply (READ CAREFULLY):

To be considered, your application must include:

-A Google Drive video link (2 minutes max) introducing yourself and outlining why you’d be a good fit for our team.

-Your experience level with GoHighLevel.

-The subject line of your message must be: "FenceUSA Operations Specialist – [Your Name]"



Applications without a voice/video recording or the correct subject line will not be considered.

title: Rockstar Paralegal Assistant
description:
Schedule: 9 AM - 5PM in the Philippines Times



We are seeking a meticulous, tech-savvy Virtual Legal Assistant to serve as the backbone of our digital organization. Your primary mission will be to ensure that every client file is complete, every data point is accurate, and our case management system reflects the current status of every matter. This is a remote, full-time position requiring extreme attention to detail and a "measure twice, cut once" mentality. Also - we are one big team looking for a long term hire to grow with us. You will be part of our team and we will meet with you regularly by zoom, with camera turned on because we want to maintain human connection with all tea
mbers.



Key Responsibilities:



Data Entry/Organization: Inputting sensitive client information into our case management software with accuracy. Names, addresses. You will make sure this information is entered in multiple places. You will review scanned documents and you will interpret from those documents where certain information should be entered.



Digital File Architecture: Organizing and maintaining cloud-based client folders (e.g., Dropbox) according to our firm’s specific naming conventions and structure.



Workflow Tracking: Updating project milestones and deadlines to ensure no client file "stalls" during the drafting or funding process. We are doing all of the work - you will help us make sure the current status of the matter is always correctly documented.



SOPs: You will create the SOPs for your own job. We will give you instructions and guidelines, but you will also create your own SOPs for how you approach the work.



AI Image Generation: You will help us format marketing materials, help us create AI educational images, etc. You will help us with Canva materials as well.



Legacy Data Cleanup: Assisting with the migration or reorganization of older files into our current streamlined system.



Qualifications & Skills:



Legal Experience: 2+ years as a legal assistant or paralegal (Estate Planning experience is a significant plus).



Software Mastery: Proficiency with legal case management software (e.g., Lawcus, Legacy Architects, Wealth Counsel) and secure cloud storage systems.



Data Integrity: A proven track record of handling high-volume data entry with zero-to-minimal error rates.



Technical Setup: Must have a secure, high-speed internet connection and a dedicated, quiet workspace that complies with client confidentiality (NDAs will be required).



Reliability: You are someone who values "rugged" reliability—you show up, do the work to a high standard, and don't require micro-management once a process is set.



How to Apply:



Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter. In your letter, please mention one specific tool or method you use to double-check your own work for data entry errors.

title: Fast Placement! VA Remote Virtual Receptionist
description:
Important: Our application process moves quickly. If you are selected to proceed to the next interview stages, you must be able to respond promptly. Candidates who do not respond in a timely manner may be passed over.



We are currently seeking a Remote Spa Receptionist to manage inbound calls and book spa appointments. This is a long-term, performance-based position. Calls are recorded and reviewed regularly. Continued placement in this role depends on accuracy, professionalism, booking success, reliable attendance, no missed calls during scheduled shifts, quick response times, and the ability to professionally manage complaints and de-escalate dissatisfied clients.



To apply: Please copy the questions below, paste them into your reply, and answer each question thoroughly.



(1) Location & Utilities: Where in the Philippines are you currently located? How often have you experienced power or internet outages in the past year? Do you have backup solutions (e.g., generator, mobile hotspot) to ensure your work is not affected?



(2) Home Setup: Will you be working from home? Is your workspace quiet and suitable for handling calls? Do you have your own computer or laptop? Please attach a screenshot of your current internet speed.



(3) Availability & Speed: Can you consistently work seven days a week, six hours per day, and remain available for the full shift without missed calls? How quickly do you typically answer inbound calls? What is your current typing speed in words per minute?



(4) Call & Complaint Experience: Describe your experience with answering inbound calls, booking or scheduling appointments, customer service, following scripts, and handling complaints. Provide one real example of how you de-escalated an upset client and the outcome.



(5) Tools & Systems: What programs, tools, phone systems, or VoIP platforms have you used for handling calls? Do you have any issues with background noise, power outages, or internet interruptions? How do you take notes and log client interactions during calls?



(6) Voice Recordings: Please submit two short voice recordings. Recording one should demonstrate booking an appointment from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM with a total amount of $60. Recording two should demonstrate handling a double charge for a 90-minute massage where the client sees two $90 charges and is requesting a refund. Your tone should be calm, polite, and professional.



(7) Current Job: Do you currently have another job? If yes, where, how many hours per week, and what are your roles and tasks in that job?



(8) Schedule, Pay & Start Date: Are you able to work seven days a week, six hours per day, during Central Standard Time (CST) hours without conflicts? What is your expected hourly or monthly rate? What is your earliest physical start date if hired?



(9) Marketing, Design & Video: Do you have experience with online marketing, creating content using Canva, or producing short video clips such as
 Reels or similar platforms? Please describe your skills and provide samples of your previous work if available.



(10) Fit, Skills & Experience: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you would be a good fit for this role. Include what you believe is most important when handling spa clients over the phone, especially during complaints, and what other skills or areas you have a lot of experience in.

title: Senior Webapp Developer SaaS NextJS/React (AI-Native)
description:
Hey, I am Michael

www.
/in/michael-bicker/



I am looking for a JavaScript Web & App Developer (AI-Native) for our team (Remote, at least 30h/week)



Company: German Forged Brands GbR

Product: Dial Dojo (AI Sales Training Platform) & other internal projects



Location: Remote

Position: Long-term contractor (full-time aquivalent)

Start Date: ASAP ;)



Who We Are:

German Forged Brands is a newly founded company building modern digital products (mostly SAAS).



One of our core products is Dial Dojo, an AI-powered sales training platform where sales professionals practice realistic conversations with AI prospects using voice technology. We are currently building and expanding the platform and are looking for an experienced JavaScript Web & App Developer to help us evolve the product.



Your Role as a Senior Developer:

As part of our development team, you will work on modern web applications and potentially mobile/desktop app integrations. You will contribute to building scalable frontend systems, integrate modern APIs and services, and work with modern AI-assisted development workflows.



Your tasks include

- Building modern web interfaces using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and TypeScript

- Developing frontend systems using frameworks such as Vue.js, React.js, or Angular

- Working with Nuxt.js for modern web applications

- Implementing responsive UI components (Bootstrap or similar frameworks)

- Integrating backend services and databases (e.g. Firebase, Supabase, MongoDB)

- Implementing authentication systems and login integrations (Google, AWS, etc.)

- Contributing to web and app development

- Implementing features for online shop systems / e-commerce workflows

- Building maintainable and scalable frontend architectures

- We use Microsoft Teams for communication, ClickUp for project management, and GitHub for code and CI/CD.

- (IMPORTANT): Using AI coding tools and AI-assisted workflows to accelerate development (we are mostly using Augment Code and Claude Code, paid accounts will be provided)



Required Skills & Experience

- at least 5 years of programing experience

- very good english verbally and written (you will need to share your vision with us in videocalls)

- Strong knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

- Solid experience with TypeScript

- Experience with modern frontend frameworks such as: Vue.js / React.js / Angular

- Experience with Nuxt.js

- Good knowledge of Bootstrap

- Hands-on experience with Firebase, Supabase, or MongoDB

- Experience with app development

- Understanding of authentication systems and login integrations

- Experience using AI coding tools and AI-supported development workflows

- Understanding of online shop / e-commerce processes



Optional but Helpful Skills

- Experience with Ionic

- Experience with Electron



What We Value

- Structured, independent, and solution-oriented work style

- Strong technical understanding of modern web and app architectures

- Ability to quickly learn new technologies and tools

- Strong interest in using AI tools to work faster and more efficiently (we are using it ALOT and try to be adaptive to it all time)

- Ownership mindset and reliability in remote work



Payments

- Payments are made via Wise directly to your bank account based on monthly invoices.



Benefits

- fixed amount of work hours, but flexible working times – results matter, not the time of day you work

- Work is tracked via ClickUp tasks

- Daily availability for quick updates (Mon–Fri) via Microsoft Teams

- 25 paid vacation days + 5 paid sick days per year

- Salary increases based on performance

- Equipment support (PC hardware, monitors, etc.) after some months of beeing with us

- Friendly remote team with experienced international contractors

- Strong learning environment



Application Steps (mandatory):

- Please apply via our application form -> 
/
/f/8caxhzj-1612/37PIVDDTR2XZNAFPXX

- Enter "SPAMCHECK" in the field "Are you an AI?"



Our hiring process usually includes:

1. Application review (we look at each candiate and gather internal feedback)

2. Video Call Interview (partly in Tagalog, because of fake profiles, scammers / fake identities)

2. A paid test week if the Video Call Interview was good

3. Signing a long-term independent contractor agreement & NDA to work together :)



Personal Note from Michael:

We are real humans, we like to give feedback, value work and people and I, especially, try to be on the edge of the newest tech. That means we are leveraging AI coding and assisted coding alot.



We’re excited to welcome you onboard :)



Best regards,

Michael Bicker



German Forged Brands GbR

title: If You Only Edit Short-Form… Don’t Apply (Long-Form Editor Needed)
description:
Most video editors can’t do this.



And that’s not an insult…



It’s just reality.





If your entire skillset is:



- Adding captions

- Zoom cuts every 2 seconds

- Throwing random B-roll on top of talking heads

- Matching words to text like karaoke



This is NOT for you.



Seriously… don’t apply.





Because what we’re building requires a completely different brain.





What we actually need (read this twice):



In short someone who can edit like this:


/watch?v=zIN5dGq5Wjs


/watch?v=D3lhrrXb4WI&t=92s



We’re looking for a long-form video editor who can:



Zoom OUT before they zoom in



Understand the point of a video before touching the timeline



Edit with intention… not decoration



Tie every cut, visual, and pacing decision back to a bigger narrative



This is NOT about making things “more engaging” with noise.



This is about making things more compelling with structure.





Here’s the problem we keep running into…



Editors who come from short-form think:



“More visuals = better edit”



So what happens?



- Every sentence gets a caption

- Every 3 seconds gets a cut

- Every idea gets drowned in unnecessary B-roll



And the final product?



Feels busy… but empty.



Looks edited… but not thought through.



We don’t want that.





What great looks like to us:



You watch a 20-minute video…



And you’re not thinking about transitions or effects…



You’re locked into the story.



Everything feels intentional.



Every cut earns its place.



The pacing breathes.



And when it ends…



You remember the message.





This role is for you if:



You’ve edited long-form content (10+ minutes) like:



- YouTube videos

- Podcast videos



And more importantly…



You think in:



- Narrative arcs

- Retention flow

- Emotional pacing

- Information hierarchy



Not just:



- Effects

- Presets

- Templates





Damaging truth:



This kind of editing is slower.



It takes more thinking.



More rewatching.



More restraint.



BUT…



The end result is 10x more powerful…



And that’s what we care about.





What you’ll be responsible for:



Taking raw long-form footage and shaping it into a cohesive, compelling story



Making editing decisions based on the goal of the video… not habits



Enhancing clarity, flow, and retention without over-editing



Knowing when to add… and when to leave things alone





What we don’t care about:



- Fancy transitions

- Overused editing tricks

- How fast you can pump out clips





What we DO care about:



- Taste



- Judgment



- Restraint



- Storytelling ability



PS. You should be able to handle basic color grading inside DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro, or Final Cut.



Nothing crazy…



But enough to make footage feel clean, consistent, and intentional.





How to apply (this is important):



Do NOT send us short-form edits.



We will ignore them.



Instead, send:



2–3 long-form projects (10+ minutes) you’ve edited



And tell us:



- What was the goal of the video?

- What editing decisions did you make to support that goal?

- What did you intentionally choose NOT to do… and why?





If you read this and thought:



“Finally… someone gets it.”



We want to see your work.





If you read this and thought:



“This seems like overthinking editing…”



This is not for you.





If this feels obvious to you…



You’re probably who we’re looking for.



Send your portfolio.

- About Young Publisher:

title: Design, Architecture & Sales Support
description:
---



**About BuiltWell Group**



BuiltWell Group is a fast-growing residential renovation general contractor based in Metro Atlanta, Georgia. We serve homeowners, real estate investors, and institutional clients across exterior and interior renovation — decks, siding, kitchens, bathrooms, flooring, full remodels, and more. We are building a business that operates differently than every other contractor in our market: faster, more professional, and more thorough at every step. We are currently expanding our back-office team to support rapid growth and are looking for a sharp, design-minded VA to become a core part of what we are building.



---



**Position: Design, Architecture & Sales Support VA (Part-Time)**

**Location:** Remote

**Hours:** 4 hours per day, Monday–Friday (20 hours per week)

**Compensation:** $400/month base + $30–$50 performance bonus per closed sale your package contributed to

**Growth:** Hours and compensation increase directly with our sales volume — this role grows as we grow



---



**What You Will Do**



Within 48 hours of every client walk, you build the presentation that closes the deal. You receive field measurements, photos, technical notes from our specialist, and scope notes from our salesperson — and you turn all of it into a professional, personalized presentation package that includes a 3D render of the finished project, three tiered pricing options, and a payment breakdown through our financing partner Hearth. You also prepare briefing documents for our salesperson before every presentation appointment and every contract appointment.



This is not a general VA role. You need architectural or design background, construction literacy, and the ability to produce clean, accurate, visually compelling work to a hard deadline every single time.



---



**Your Core Deliverables**



- 3D renders — 2 polished, photorealistic variations per project specific to the client's home and style

- Three-tier presentation package — Good / Better / Best with full scope, pricing, inclusions, and exclusions per tier

- Payment breakdown — Hearth monthly estimates for each tier based on the client's current funding status

- Appointment 2 briefing — delivered the evening before, covering client priorities, recommended lead tier, financing status, and anticipated objections

- Appointment 3 briefing — delivered the morning of, covering chosen tier, funding confirmation, deposit structure, and contract scope

- Design asset library — before/after renders and marketing graphics for every completed project



---



**What You Need**



- Background in architecture, interior design, construction management, or a closely related field — required

- Strong visual design sense and Canva Pro proficiency

- Experience with AI rendering tools (REimagineHome, Cedreo, or similar) or strong ability to learn quickly

- Knowledge of renovation materials and finish levels — you know the difference between standard, mid-grade, and premium

- Hard deadline reliability — 5pm on production day is a promise, not a target

- Availability during US Eastern business hours, specifically Wednesday and Friday afternoons for approval windows

- Fluent written English



---



**The Performance Structure**



Your base compensation is $400 per month for 20 hours per week. Every sale that closes from a package you built earns you a $30–$50 bonus on top of that. There is no cap. As BuiltWell's private renovation volume grows — which it is doing aggressively — your hours increase first, then your rate. The right person for this role will not be at 20 hours per week for long.



---



**To Apply**



Send us a brief paragraph on your design or architectural background, one example of a presentation or visual project you have produced, and your availability. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a paid 48-hour skills test — a real intake form, a real brief, and a real deadline. That test is the interview.



---

title: HubSpot Configuration Expert 3 hires - Remote, AU Timezone
description:
Hey there,



We're a delivery consultancy that implements HubSpot for clients across Australia and New Zealand. Our projects range from CRM setup to full multi-hub configurations with integrations, automations, and custom reporting.



We're growing and need three HubSpot specialists to join our implementation team.



What You'll Be Doing:



Configuring HubSpot portals based on detailed implementation documentation

Creating and managing custom properties, pipelines, deal stages, and lifecycle stages

Building workflows, sequences, and automation rules

Setting up reports, dashboards, and list segmentation

Configuring integrations between HubSpot and third-party tools

Following step-by-step implementation guides (every task comes with clear documentation, you won't be guessing)



What We're Looking For:



1-3 years of hands-on HubSpot configuration experience (not just certifications)

Comfortable working across Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub, and Operations Hub

Understands HubSpot objects, properties, associations, and how data flows between them

Can read implementation documentation and execute without constant supervision

Reliable, communicative, and comfortable asking questions when something is unclear

Available to work Australian business hours (AEST/AEDT)

Nice to Have



HubSpot certifications (Solutions Architecture, Revenue Operations, etc.)

Experience with HubSpot API or developer tools

Familiarity with tools like N8N or native integrations

Engagement Details



Contractor arrangement (you invoice monthly, paid via Wise)

Consistent weekly hours, ongoing work

Competitive monthly rate



To Apply



Please include the following in your application:



A brief summary of the HubSpot portals you've configured (which hubs, what size company, what you built)

Answer this: You receive a task to create a deal pipeline with 5 stages, auto-assignment to the deal owner based on the associated company owner, and a workflow that moves deals to "Closed Lost" if no activity for 30 days. Walk us through exactly how you'd set this up in HubSpot, step by step.

Your availability and timezone confirmation. We're looking forward to hearing from you.
